definition: Cannot recover wounds or receive healing.
duration: Lasts 1 minute

definition: Physical Disadvantage.
duration: Lasts 6 weeks

definition: Mental Disadvantage; Spellcasting Disadvantage.
duration: Until a Long Rest

definition: Mental Disadvantage.
duration: Until a Press On or irritant is removed

definition: Physical Disadvantage.
duration: Off-Action Survival Check, or until a Long Rest

definition: Cannot move; restrained by an object or creature.
duration: Off-Action Physical Check

definition: Disadvantage on ranged attacks; advantage for melee attacks against you.
duration: Requires 15ft of movement to stand

definition: Movement halved.
duration: Until source is removed or until the end of your next turn

definition: Disadvantage on attack checks; attacks against you have advantage.
duration: Off-Action removal unless permanent

definition: Mental Disadvantage; cannot attack the source of the charm.
duration: Ends based on the charm's source

definition: Mental Disadvantage; attacks against you have +1.
duration: Until end of next turn

definition: Stealth Disadvantage; Provoke Disadvantage.
duration: Until end of next turn

definition: Must dash away or hide until the end of your next turn. Cannot be triggered by the same source twice.
duration: Ends if you cannot see the source until the start of your next turn

definition: Immune to physical damage. Cannot attack non-intangible creatures. Cannot move through inorganic material. Half Movement.
duration: Ends after the effect's duration or through magical disruption

definition: Cannot be targeted by Provoke; checks against you have disadvantage.
duration: Ends after making an aggressive action or when revealed by powder or oil

definition: Cannot make attack actions; attacks against you have advantage.
duration: Off-Action removal if possible; escape from restraints

definition: Character dies and cannot interact with the living world.
duration: An ally may touch you before the end of their next turn and make a Mental check to return you to Injured. Otherwise only resurrection magic can remove this condition.

definition: -3 on all checks; movement halved.
duration: Persists until a Long Rest, magical recovery, or specific intervention

definition: -3 on all checks; movement halved.
duration: Persists until a Long Rest, magical recovery, or specific intervention

definition: Take double damage.
duration: Until end of next turn

definition: At 0 wounds, any further damage causes immediate Death.
duration: Healing removes the condition. Excess damage does not carry over unless the Narrator determines instant death.

definition: -3 on all checks; movement halved.
duration: Until end of next turn or Press On

definition: Unable to act; vulnerable to critical hits and Finishers.
duration: Ends after a set duration, damage taken, or Off-Action ally assistance

definition: Precision, reflexes, and physical coordination. Each point increases Wounds. Movement: base 30ft, +5ft per 2 points.
Transform raw materials into tools, weapons, or art. Covers repair and tinkering.
Move quietly and remain unnoticed - hiding, blending into crowds, or shadowing targets without detection.
Raw physical power and resilience. Each point increases Wounds.
Primal instinct for navigating danger and enduring harsh environments. Governs breath-holding: 3 rounds + Survival.
Persuasion, emotional intelligence, and performance. Used to sway opinions, deceive, negotiate, and command.
Comprehension of magic, research, and knowledge. Covers arcana, history, nature, science, and occult.
The unseen hand of fate. Governs probability, fortunate timing, and chance-based outcomes.
Perceiving surroundings and reading people. Low-light vision: base 30ft, +5ft per 2 points.
A mystical link to the unseen. Every 2 points grants 1 Max Mana.
Your total hit capacity. Equal to Agility + Strength (minimum 1). Reaching 0 applies the Injured condition.
Absorbs damage before wounds. Repaired via Crafting on Press On, or restored to at least half on Long Rest.
Magical energy used to cast spells. Every 2 Spirit grants 1 Max Mana. Restored on Press On and Long Rest.
A limited per-class pool that powers special abilities. Replenished by Press On or Long Rest.
A class resource charge used to amplify effects. Spending it triggers stronger outcomes in certain abilities.
Measures the weight and encumbrance of carried items. Exceeding your Strength in Bulk causes Pinned; exceeding 2x Strength adds Exhaustion.
A scale from -5 (Hostile) to +5 (Devoted) tracking trust with NPCs and factions. Starts at 0 (Neutral). Shifts through meaningful actions, gifts, and betrayals.
Roll a d20+4 and a d20, take the higher result.
Roll a d20-4 and a d20, take the lower result.
Having double Disadvantage on a roll means you cannot perform that action at all.
Two characters each roll and add their relevant checks; the higher total wins.
The whole party attempts the same action. Total successes needed equal the number of participants.
An extreme high roll. In combat: double damage, a bonus damage table roll, or instantly finishing a weakened foe. Outside combat: perfect timing or bonus information.
An extreme low roll. In combat: half damage, a weapon slip, or a new complication. Spells may backfire or fizzle.
Non-Action: take 4 stress damage to gain +3 on a low roll.
At each Path Progression Step, gain one of: +1 to any check (max 15), +1 Mana, or +1 Class Resource.
Triggered when a creature misses an attack or willingly moves out of melee range without Disengaging. Grants the target a counterattack.
Determines turn order in combat. Roll a d20 + Agility; highest goes first.
A check made by enemies and NPCs when threatened. A failed check may cause them to flee combat.
Declare before attacking, then accept -10 to hit. On success, inflict a specific condition instead of normal damage.
Instantly eliminate a helpless, sleeping, or completely unaware foe without full combat. Cannot be used on an active or aware opponent.
Half cover: Disadvantage to hit the target. Full cover: target cannot be targeted at all.
When attacking from stealth, gain one extra action on the first turn. Reveals your position unless an ability says otherwise.
A damage result that pushes the target a set distance (10ft or 20ft depending on the source).
A damage result that causes your attack to also strike creatures adjacent to your original target.
A damage result that lets you take a Stance, repositioning your combat advantage.
Knock a weapon from a creature's hand. As a Half-Action: make an opposed Strength or Agility check. Also a possible damage result from Slashing and Thunder attacks.
A damage result: deal half the rolled damage and restore that amount to yourself as wounds.
A damage result: a slain creature rises under the attacker's control or as an undead.
A damage result: force evil or undead creatures to be banished or driven back from the area.
The primary activity on your turn - attacking, casting a spell, etc. Generally one per turn.
A smaller action. Two Half-Actions can replace one full Action.
A quick reaction usable on or off your turn without spending your main action.
Minor tasks or brief communication that do not consume any part of your action economy.
Make a melee or ranged attack against a creature or object within range.
Action or Off-Action: Make an Agility or Strength check to deal 2 Impact damage.
Use your action to double your movement distance for the turn.
Make an opposed Strength check to impose the Pinned condition on a creature.
Requires sound, movement, and a spellcasting check (usually Spirit). Costs Mana.
Give a potion to a downed ally as a full action. Drinking a potion yourself is a Half-Action.
Move out of a creature's melee range without triggering Provoke.
Give a nearby creature +1 to their next check.

Choose a combat stance lasting until your next turn: Advantage Stance (advantage on next attack), Disadvantage Stance (enemies attacking you or nearby allies suffer disadvantage), Offensive Stance (use Off-Action to Provoke), or Reaction Stance (react to failed attacks against you).
A special action taken out of turn, such as an opportunity attack or a response to a trigger.
Reduce incoming melee damage by half using a shield.
Impose Disadvantage on an enemy's attack with a successful check.
Force enemies to make a Morale Check using an Influence (Intimidate) roll.
Drink or hand off a potion as an Off-Action.
After combat: regain 1 class resource and 1 Mana, repair armor (1d6 + Crafting), heal (1d6 + Survival), and remove all conditions lasting 1 minute or less. Usable twice per day, not consecutively.
10 hours of uninterrupted downtime. Fully restores wounds, class resources, and Mana. Repairs armor to at least half. Removes conditions lasting one day or less.
5ft or touching.
10ft.
Within 50ft.
Within line of sight.
Requires twice as much movement; every 5ft costs 10ft.
Take 5 impact damage per 10ft fallen. A fall that brings you to Injured causes the Death condition instead.
While underground, immune to sight-based spells and non-piercing weapons. Requires a way to breathe.
Move to another unoccupied space without provoking. Only available to certain creatures or abilities.
Less than 1ft. Examples: insects, pixies, rings.
1-4ft. Examples: gnomes, goblins, Klienkin.
5-7ft. Examples: humans, beastfolk.
8-15ft. Examples: ogres, dire bears, golem enforcers.
15-25ft. Examples: clockwork titans.
25-100ft. Examples: war zeppelins, cathedral domes.
Over 100ft. Examples: floating citadels, mountain elementals.
The game master in Check20. Creates the world, controls NPCs, runs scenes, and responds to player actions.
The world in which Check20 takes place - a 1920s-inspired high-fantasy setting blending magic with industrialization.
The in-world unit of distance, abbreviated ft. Slightly smaller than a foot. Use "ferns" at the table for short.
A character's chosen specialization within their Class. Defines the step-by-step progression of abilities.
A further specialization within a Path, fine-tuning a character's role and unique capabilities.
Time between adventures used for training, business, recovery, and personal projects. Especially important for Merchant characters.
Can be wielded in one hand, leaving the other hand free.
Requires both hands to use. You cannot use a shield or hold another item while wielding it.
Can be used with one or two hands. Using two hands provides no additional benefit unless otherwise stated.
Can be used as an Off-Action.
You can carry up to 2 Hefty items before becoming Pinned.
Can attack targets up to 10 ft away.
Can be thrown as a ranged attack, usually at Short range.
Returns to your hand immediately after being thrown.
Easy to conceal. Creatures must succeed on an Observation check to find it on your person.
After firing, you must spend any action type to reload before firing again.
Attack checks against targets in Melee range are made with disadvantage.
Worn or strapped to the body. Does not occupy a hand slot and cannot be disarmed.
Can be used without occupying either hand, such as a mounted weapon.
Produces a loud gunshot that may attract attention or trigger environmental reactions.
Makes significant noise when used and automatically breaks stealth.
Tied to the Tech Origin and may interact with abilities or effects that target Tech items.
Your first attack against an unaware target has advantage.
